mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede corporate revenue contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as earnings revisions turn adverse.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points within 3 months
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in fewer than 40% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising consumer credit balances and falling housing starts indicates a shift from durable goods investment to consumption-driven demand, which historically supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a negative coefficient in a rolling 3-year regression, or the combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5% while the consumer price index exceeds 230, the Federal Reserve maintains restrictive policy that suppresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns under this dual-threshold regime are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or the joint signal's information ratio falls below 0.2 over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP exceeding 2.5% annualized rate reflects robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week as earnings momentum confirms.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 5-day forward returns following GDP beats are negative in more than 60% of observations across three consecutive business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index below 95.0 indicates weakening manufacturing output and supply chain contraction, which precedes margin compression and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month window.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 95.0 and remains below for two consecutive months
failure_condition: The signal yields positive 20-day returns in over 50% of triggered episodes, or the rolling Spearman correlation between INDPRO declines and SPX returns exceeds 0.10 in the wrong direction.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 per month and a declining unemployment rate signals a self-reinforcing labor demand expansion that boosts consumer spending and equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive information ratio over any 3-year rolling window, or forward 60-day returns under these conditions are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP growth below 1.0% annualized rate signals an aggregate demand shock that forces corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week as market participants price in recession risk.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if 5-day forward returns following sub-1% GDP growth are positive in more than 45% of instances across two decades of data, or the predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.
